user.title: EV Trip Planner - Vehicle Setup
user.description: Configure your electric vehicle for trip planning

data_description.vehicle_name: A friendly name for your vehicle (e.g., 'Chispitas', 'Morgan')
data_description.vehicle_type: Select the type of vehicle you want to configure

sensors.title: Vehicle Sensors
sensors.description: Select the sensors for battery monitoring

data_description.soc_sensor: Sensor that reports State of Charge as percentage (e.g., sensor.ovms_soc). Should show values like 78%, 45%, etc.
data_description.battery_capacity: Total battery capacity in kWh (e.g., 27 for Nissan Leaf, 52 for Tesla Model 3). Example: 40
data_description.charging_power: Maximum charging power in kW (e.g., 7.2 for single-phase, 11 for three-phase). Example: 7.2
data_description.range_sensor: Optional: Sensor that reports remaining range in km or miles (e.g., sensor.ovms_range). Used for additional monitoring.
data_description.charging_status: Optional: Binary sensor that indicates if vehicle is charging (e.g., binary_sensor.ovms_charging). Shows 'on' when charging.

data_description.consumption: Average energy consumption per km (e.g., 0.15 for 15 kWh/100km). Higher values = more energy needed. Example: 0.15
data_description.safety_margin: Extra battery percentage to keep as buffer (e.g., 10 = always keep 10% charge). Prevents running out of battery. Example: 10
data_description.control_type: How should charging be controlled? 'Notifications Only (no control)' means no automatic control, just alerts.

emhass.title: EMHASS Integration (Optional)
emhass.description: Configure EMHASS integration for smart charging optimization. This step is optional - you can skip it and configure later.

data_description.planning_horizon: Number of days EMHASS should plan ahead (1-30 days). Usually matches your EMHASS day-ahead optimization horizon. Example: 7
data_description.planning_sensor: Optional: If your optimizer publishes a sensor with planning horizon, select it here. Otherwise leave empty and use manual value above.
data_description.max_deferrable_loads: Maximum number of simultaneous deferrable loads EMHASS is configured for (10-100). Usually 50. Example: 50

presence.title: Presence Detection (Optional)
presence.description: Configure presence detection to prevent charging when vehicle is away. This step is optional - you can skip it.

data_description.home_sensor: Optional: Binary sensor that indicates if vehicle is at home (e.g., binary_sensor.chispitas_home). Shows 'on' when vehicle is home.
data_description.plugged_sensor: Optional: Binary sensor that indicates if charging cable is connected (e.g., binary_sensor.chispitas_plugged). Shows 'on' when plugged.
data_description.home_coordinates: Optional: Your home coordinates as 'latitude, longitude' (e.g., '40.1234, -3.5678'). Used if no home sensor available.
data_description.vehicle_coordinates_sensor: Optional: Sensor with vehicle GPS coordinates (e.g., sensor.ovms_location). Used with home coordinates for distance calculation.
data_description.notification_service: Service to call for notifications (e.g., notify.mobile_app). Used to alert when charging needed but not possible.

error.invalid_planning_horizon: Planning horizon must be between 1 and 30 days
error.invalid_max_deferrable_loads: Maximum deferrable loads must be between 10 and 100
error.home_sensor_not_found: The selected home sensor was not found. Please check the entity ID.
error.plugged_sensor_not_found: The selected plugged sensor was not found. Please check the entity ID.
